symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive dysphagia to solid foods over ~4 weeks
symptoms.character: solid foods (bread, meat) catching and sticking, worsening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Solid-predominant, progressive dysphagia is the hallmark of a fixed mechanical narrowing.
Progressive solid dysphagia in an older adult is a cardinal alarm feature that mandates endoscopic exclusion of cancer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute food bolus sensation (food lodged in lower chest, will not pass)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute solid-food lodging at a known point of narrowing is the defining feature of bolus impaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regurgitation of water and all swallowed fluids (cannot tolerate oral intake)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to pass even fluids indicates a high-grade obstruction and is the patient-reportable time-critical feature driving urgent escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic retrosternal burning discomfort (reflux) for 15 years

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Long-standing acid reflux is the mechanism for peptic stricture and Barrett's metaplasia.
Chronic GORD is the principal risk factor for Barrett's metaplasia.
Pain provoked by swallowing with a food-sticking sensation points to an oesophageal rather than cardiac origin.
Retrosternal pain keeps cardiac causes on the list, but swallowing provocation and regurgitation argue against a primary cardiac event; cannot be excluded remotely.
Nocturnal cough is a recognised extra-oesophageal manifestation of reflux/microaspiration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Able to swallow own saliva without drooling (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Complete oesophageal obstruction with imminent aspiration
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ability to manage secretions argues against a complete obstruction and supports urgent (ED) rather than emergency-ambulance-level triage — but does not remove the need for urgent endoscopic assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haematemesis, melaena, or unintentional weight loss (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bleeding and weight loss lowers pre-test probability but does NOT exclude malignancy — endoscopy + biopsy remains mandatory.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR ~95) and blood pressure ~145/85 measured by clinician

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia and mild hypertension are non-specific, consistent with pain and anxiety; a sealed clinician measurement not available to the AI Doctor via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear oropharynx with no saliva pooling, midline trachea, no stridor, chest clear (clinician examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring in-person airway findings — sealed clinician examination the AI Doctor cannot perform remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Upper GI endoscopy: salmon-pink columnar mucosa with distal stricture, biopsy for histology

investigation_details.expected_result: Distal oesophageal stricture with salmon-pink Barrett's-type mucosa; biopsy to confirm metaplasia and exclude dysplasia/malignancy
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The bolus is not cleared and benign versus malignant stricture cannot be distinguished — missed cancer and unrelieved obstruction.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Endoscopy with biopsy is the definitive test for Barrett's, stricture, and malignancy — never available in real time via telehealth.
Only endoscopic biopsy can exclude a malignant stricture.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute food bolus impaction on a peptic stricture
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Benign peptic oesophageal stricture with food bolus impaction
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic reflux + progressive solid dysphagia + acute bolus + inability to pass fluids together localise a fixed distal narrowing with acute obstruction requiring urgent endoscopic assessment.
Progressive solid dysphagia in an older adult with long-standing reflux mandates urgent endoscopy to exclude oesophageal cancer, regardless of the benign-sounding history.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent upper GI endoscopy (gastroscopy) with biopsy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Both therapeutic (disimpaction, stricture assessment/dilatation) and diagnostic (biopsy to exclude malignancy and confirm Barrett's) — the single most important investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT chest / contrast study if perforation suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Indicated only if features of oesophageal perforation develop (sudden severe chest/back pain, surgical emphysema, sepsis).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: ECG and troponin to exclude cardiac cause if diagnostic doubt
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Retrosternal chest pain warrants cardiac screening when the oesophageal cause is not certain, particularly in an older adult.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Barium swallow in the acute impaction setting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Contraindicated during acute impaction with regurgitation — aspiration risk and it obscures subsequent endoscopy.
